1992-93.champion: Manchester United's first Premier League title — Cantona's influence after Jan transfer.

1993-94.champion: Utd dominant, Man United win the Double. 92pt record at the time.

1994-95.champion: Blackburn's only title, Alan Shearer's 34 goals, Dalglish's achievement.

1995-96.champion: Cantona's chip goal vs Wimbledon. Newcastle's 12-point lead squandered.

1996-97.champion: Man Utd 4th title in 5 years. Shearer joins Newcastle for £15m record fee.

1997-98.champion: Arsenal's Double under Wenger. Bergkamp's wonder goal vs Leicester.

topScorer.name: Chris Sutton / Michael Owen / Dion Dublin

1998-99.champion: Treble-winning Man Utd. Schmeichel's last season.

1999-00.champion: Man Utd's record 91pts — Yorke & Cole lethal partnership.

2000-01.champion: Utd three-in-a-row. Van Nistelrooy joins the following summer.

2001-02.champion: Arsenal's Invincibles dry run. Henry at his peak. Double again.

2002-03.champion: Man Utd 8th PL title. van Nistelrooy 25 goals.

2003-04.champion: Arsenal Invincibles — 38 games unbeaten. Greatest PL season.

2004-05.champion: Chelsea's 95pts record under Mourinho. Lampard 13 goals from midfield.

2005-06.champion: Chelsea back-to-back. Lampard runner-up Ballon d'Or.

2006-07.champion: Man Utd return — Ronaldo emerging. Rooney & Ronaldo up front.

2007-08.champion: Man Utd champions & UCL. Ronaldo 31 PL goals, 42 total.

2008-09.champion: Utd 3rd in a row. Liverpool 2nd — Gerrard & Torres brilliant.

2009-10.champion: Chelsea 103 goals. Drogba golden boot. Ancelotti's first title.

2011-12.champion: Aguero's 93:20. City beat Utd on goal difference in final minutes.

2012-13.champion: Van Persie joins Utd — 26 goals. Ferguson's final season.

2013-14.champion: City win by 2pts from Liverpool. Suarez 31 goals. Gerrard slip.

2014-15.champion: Chelsea under Mourinho. Costa/Hazard/Fabregas trio dominant.

2015-16.champion: Leicester's miracle — 5000-1 odds. Vardy & Mahrez inspirational.

2017-18.champion: Man City's 100pt season — all-time PL record. 19pt winning margin.

2018-19.champion: City 98pts pip Liverpool 97pts — closest title race ever.

topScorer.name: Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang / Mohamed Salah / Sadio Mané

2019-20.champion: Liverpool's first league title in 30 years. 99pts, COVID-affected.

2020-21.champion: Man City dominant second half of season. Gündoğan 13 goals.

2021-22.champion: City edge out Liverpool by 1pt. Salah / Son share golden boot.

2022-23.champion: City's 5th title in 6 years. Haaland 36 goals — obliterated record.

2023-24.champion: City 4-in-a-row. Arsenal agonisingly close. Haaland 27 goals.

2024-25.champion: Liverpool's title under Arne Slot. Salah golden boot farewell season.
